questions.question: Which of the following operating systems is NOT listed as a supported platform for Notion Calendar?

options.text: Mac
options.explanation: Mac is explicitly listed as a supported platform in the Download section.

options.text: Windows
options.explanation: Windows is explicitly listed as a supported platform in the Download section.

options.text: Linux
options.explanation: Linux is not mentioned in the list of supported platforms (Mac, Windows, Web, iOS, Android).

options.text: iOS
options.explanation: iOS is explicitly listed as a supported platform in the Download section.

questions.question: What happens if you sign up for Notion Calendar using an email address that doesn't already have a Notion account?

options.text: You will be redirected to create a new Google account.
options.explanation: Notion Calendar creates a new Notion account for you, not a Google account, using the email address you provided.

options.text: You can only use it for one month before you need to link a paid plan.
options.explanation: The article does not mention a time limit on free accounts; it focuses on account creation and linking.

options.text: A Notion account will be created for you using the same email address.
options.explanation: The article states: 'If you don't already have a Notion account, no problem — when you sign up for Notion Calendar, a Notion account will be created for you using the same email address.'

options.text: You must create a separate Notion account in a different browser.
options.explanation: Notion handles the account creation automatically during the signup process.

questions.question: According to the Google permissions section, which of the following is true regarding user data?

options.text: Notion Calendar can access your restricted data if you approve it.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Notion Calendar is verified by Google and cannot access user data categorized as restricted.

options.text: Notion Calendar never sees your Google password.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'We’ll never see your Google password.'

options.text: Google permissions allow Notion Calendar to edit your calendar without your action.
options.explanation: Permissions are only used to access data on your behalf; direct action from you is always required for edits.

options.text: Notion Calendar can access your restricted data if you provide explicit consent.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Notion Calendar is verified by Google and cannot access any user data categorized as restricted.

questions.question: When connecting an iCloud account to Notion Calendar, what specific type of password is required?

options.text: Your standard Apple ID password.
options.explanation: Apple blocks access to standard passwords for third-party apps to protect security. You must use an app-specific password.

options.text: A two-factor authentication code.
options.explanation: While Two-Factor Authentication is generally used, the article specifically highlights the need for an 'app-specific password' to log in.

options.text: An app-specific password.
options.explanation: The article states: 'Apple requires you to use an app-specific password for Notion Calendar to connect your iCloud account.'

options.text: A password created in the Notion Calendar settings.
options.explanation: App-specific passwords are created within your Apple account settings, not in Notion Calendar.

questions.question: Why might you receive an authorization error when signing in with a work email address?

options.text: Because you entered the wrong password.
options.explanation: While a wrong password is a common issue, the article specifically links authorization errors with work emails to organizational settings, not just password entry.

options.text: Because your browser is outdated.
options.explanation: Browser issues are not mentioned as the cause for authorization errors in the troubleshooting section.

options.text: Because an admin hasn't approved Notion Calendar to be authorized with Google at your organization.
options.explanation: The article states: 'it might be because an admin hasn't approved Notion Calendar to be authorized with Google at your organization yet.'

options.text: Because you are using an iOS device.
options.explanation: The authorization error is specific to Google Workspace settings, not the device type.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of Notion accounts that can be linked to a single Notion Calendar account?

options.text: Unlimited accounts.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'Only one Notion account can be linked to your Notion Calendar account.'

options.text: Two accounts.
options.explanation: While you can connect multiple calendars and workspaces, only one primary account can be linked.

options.text: One account.
options.explanation: The article states: 'Only one Notion account can be linked to your Notion Calendar account.'

options.text: Five accounts.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'Only one Notion account can be linked to your Notion Calendar account.'

questions.question: How is an app-specific password formatted for Notion Calendar?

options.text: A single string of 16 random alphanumeric characters.
options.explanation: The article specifies the format: 'four groups of four letters, like abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop.'

options.text: A single string of 12 random alphanumeric characters.
options.explanation: The article specifies the format: 'four groups of four letters, like abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop.'

options.text: Four groups of four letters (e.g., abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop).
options.explanation: The article states: 'this password should consist of four groups of four letters, like abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop.'

options.text: A 24-character hexadecimal string.
options.explanation: The article specifies the format: 'four groups of four letters, like abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop.'

questions.question: If you are unable to log in with your Apple account, what should you verify?

options.text: That you are using your app-specific password for Notion Calendar.
options.explanation: The troubleshooting section advises: 'make sure you’re using your app-specific password for Notion Calendar and not your Apple account password.'

options.text: That your VPN connection is stable.
options.explanation: VPN stability is not mentioned as a cause for Apple login issues in the article.

options.text: That your Apple ID password is at least 8 characters long.
options.explanation: The issue is not password length, but rather the type of password required (app-specific vs. standard).

options.text: That you have enabled Two-Factor Authentication.
options.explanation: While TFA is generally good practice, the article specifically points to the app-specific password as the solution for login failures.

questions.question: What happens if you lose access to the Notion account linked to your Notion Calendar?

options.text: You can change the linked account instantly in the settings menu.
options.explanation: You cannot change the linked account instantly. If you lose access, you must fill out a form to request a change.

options.text: You will lose access to Notion Calendar.
options.explanation: The article states that if you lose access to the primary account, you will also lose access to your Notion Calendar.

options.text: You can still access it using a secondary account.
options.explanation: Only one account can be linked at a time. If the primary account is inaccessible, the Calendar is inaccessible.

options.text: You can request a refund for the subscription.
options.explanation: The article does not mention refunds for account access issues.

questions.question: If you need to change your linked Notion account, what is the process?

options.text: Log out and log back in with the new email address.
options.explanation: The article states this is not possible. You must fill out a support form to change the linked account.

options.text: Fill out a specific form and wait for a response within 72 hours.
options.explanation: The article directs users to 'fill out this form' and states they will 'respond within 72 hours of the submission.'

options.text: Delete your current account and sign up with a new one.
options.explanation: The article describes a process for changing the linked account without necessarily deleting the existing one, involving a support request.

options.text: Contact your Google Workspace admin to request the change.
options.explanation: While admins manage Google app authorization, the article specifies that users should fill out a form for account changes.
